skill.name: maxhub-hybrid
skill.description: Uses the MaxHub HybridParser API to parse video links and extract structured public content data through configured HTTPS requests.
skill.summary: Parse hybrid video URLs into structured public content data with MaxHub.
skill.author: MaxHub Team

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backticks, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and high entropy are mostly false positives from Markdown code fences, API tables, placeholders, and Chinese text. The confirmed risk is legitimate but sensitive: the skill directs agents to send MAXHUB_API_KEY in HTTPS API requests to a configured MaxHub base URL. No evidence of prompt injection, hidden payloads, local file access, credential harvesting beyond the declared API key, or malicious exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Credential-Bearing Network Requests
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to call the MaxHub API over HTTPS and send MAXHUB_API_KEY in the x-api-key header. This is declared behavior, but users must verify MAXHUB_BASE_URL before use because a malicious or mistaken base URL would receive the API key.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly declares MAXHUB_API_KEY and MAXHUB_BASE_URL, then shows requests that attach the key to API calls. The behavior appears intentional and documented, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Shell Curl Examples With User-Supplied URL Parameters
medium_findings.description: The Markdown examples show curl commands for API calls, including a video URL query parameter. If an agent executes similar shell commands with unencoded user input, the API key could be exposed to unintended endpoints or logs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The curl commands are documentation examples rather than executable scripts. Risk depends on an agent copying them into a shell and interpolating untrusted values without encoding.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Backtick and Reconnaissance Static Hits
low_findings.description: Static detections for Ruby backticks and system reconnaissance are false positives caused by Markdown code fences, inline code, API catalog entries, and ordinary descriptive text. No Ruby execution or host reconnaissance logic is present in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are tables, code fences, or prose. I did not find executable Ruby code, host inventory commands, or local system probing.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Entropy and Weak Cryptography Static Hits
low_findings.description: High entropy and weak cryptography detections appear to be caused by multilingual Markdown content and words such as HybridParser. No encoded payload, encrypted blob, hash routine, or cryptographic implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are plain Markdown and render as readable documentation. I did not find hidden binary content, obfuscated code, or weak crypto usage.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Key Passed Through Curl Header
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented curl pattern sends MAXHUB_API_KEY in an x-api-key header. This is required for the service, but it should only be used with a trusted HTTPS MAXHUB_BASE_URL.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is explicit and repeated in the documentation. It is not malicious by itself, but it is a meaningful credential-handling pattern.

content.user_title: Parse Hybrid Video Data with MaxHub
content.value_statement: Teams often need structured data from public video links without building a custom parser. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to call the MaxHub HybridParser API with clear limits and credential handling.
actual_capabilities: Identifies when a user needs HybridParser video data extraction.
Documents the GET endpoint for hybrid video data lookup.
Shows how to pass MAXHUB_API_KEY through the x-api-key header.
Uses MAXHUB_BASE_URL so deployments can target the configured MaxHub host.
Defines pagination, batch, and chain-depth limits for controlled API usage.
Explains how to avoid collecting sensitive personal privacy data.
limitations: Requires a valid MaxHub account and API key before use.
Only one confirmed HybridParser endpoint is documented in this skill.
Does not include executable client code or a typed SDK.
API results depend on MaxHub availability, quotas, and source platform access.

use_cases.title: Extract Public Video Metadata
use_cases.description: Parse a public video URL and return structured fields for review, tagging, or reporting.

use_cases.title: Review Parser Integration Steps
use_cases.description: Understand the required endpoint, header, base URL, and parameter before wiring MaxHub into an internal workflow.

use_cases.title: Control API Usage Costs
use_cases.description: Apply the documented page, batch, depth, and call-count limits before running repeated parsing tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Parse One Video Link
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the MaxHub HybridParser skill to parse this public video URL. Return the available structured fields and note any missing data.

prompt_templates.title: Explain Required API Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain what environment variables and request headers I need before using the HybridParser endpoint. Include safety checks for the base URL.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Small Batch Parse
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a batch parse for these public video links. Keep the batch within the skill limits and estimate the number of API calls.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Parsing Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this proposed HybridParser workflow for API cost, credential exposure, privacy concerns, and rate-limit risk before execution.

output_examples.input: Parse one public video URL with HybridParser.
output_examples.output: A concise result summary with title, creator, public engagement fields, source URL status, and missing fields if the API omits them.

output_examples.input: Prepare a batch parse for eight public links.
output: Credential and base URL safety note

output_examples.output: A troubleshooting note covering invalid URL, missing API key, quota limits, unsupported source platform, or upstream API errors.

best_practices: Verify MAXHUB_BASE_URL points to the expected trusted HTTPS host before sending an API key.
Encode user-provided video URLs before placing them in query parameters.
Confirm batch size, expected call count, and cost before running repeated parses.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys directly into prompts, chat logs, or shared documents.
Do not use the parser for private, sensitive, or unauthorized personal data collection.
Do not run large batches without checking quota, rate limits, and user approval.

faq.question: What does this skill parse?
faq.answer: It focuses on public video URL parsing through the MaxHub HybridParser video data endpoint.

faq.question: Does it need an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill expects MAXHUB_API_KEY to be available as an environment variable.

faq.question: Does it store credentials?
faq.answer: The documentation says credentials are passed through request headers and are not stored by the skill.

faq.question: Can it call any API host?
faq.answer: It uses MAXHUB_BASE_URL. Users should set this only to a trusted HTTPS MaxHub host.

faq.question: Is this safe for bulk scraping?
faq.answer: No. The skill includes limits and privacy guidance, and it should only be used for authorized public data.

faq.question: Does it include runnable software?
faq.answer: No. It is Markdown guidance with endpoint details, examples, safety rules, and workflow patterns.
